mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0% for at least two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates accelerating manufacturing output and supply chain utilization, generating short-term positive market momentum and S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns in >60% of early-cycle recovery periods, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% monthly change.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 230.0 reflects entrenched inflationary pressures that erode real corporate margins and force eventual monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >50% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 230.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate profit growth, which directly translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns (p > 0.05) across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant industrial production alongside rising price levels indicates supply-side bottlenecks and stagflationary risks, which compress equity valuations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of flat INDPRO and rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative returns with >55% accuracy in historical stagflation periods.
